note: LIVE INCIDENT BANNER added under the V11 hard-fork section. Block 6,989 has been outstanding for ~80 minutes (chain tip stuck at 6,988). Diagnostic verified: phase2_active=false (Phase 2 has NOT activated prematurely — phase2_height=7,100 confirmed via getlotterystate), no chain split (getbestblockhash on production node matches public tip 29805b9e91...33d877), zero rejects/invalids/forks/signature-failures in journalctl over the last 90 min, 7 peers connected, mempool clean, production binary md5 matches the build that passed all 14/14 critical V11 Phase 1 + Phase 2 tests. Most likely cause: coordinated update window where multiple operators simultaneously pull/rebuild/restart, dropping effective network hashrate temporarily; combined with Poisson variance on a difficulty-locked block at the cASERT cascade floor (E7, drop=46 levels), intervals can stretch to 1.5–2h. This is operationally expected, not a consensus fault. Rollback contingency: if block 6,989 has not landed by 150 minutes total elapsed (~70 min from now), the production node's sost-node binary will be reverted to /opt/sost/build/sost-node.pre-phase2-7100.bak while diagnostics continue; V11_PHASE2_HEIGHT in source stays at 7,100. Probability of reaching the 150-min threshold from variance alone ≈ e^(-12) ≈ 1 in 165,000 — at which point a real operational issue is presumed. Miners: keep your miner running, do NOT manually roll back individually, the coordinated rollback (if needed) is executed by ops on the seed node only. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
